title: jasminum coarctatum var. vanprukii
intro: Jasminum coarctatum var. vanprukii, also known as Thai Jasmine, is a species of flowering plant in the family Oleaceae, native to Thailand. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which contribute to its potential health benefits, such as reducing stress and improving sleep quality. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, including teas and infusions, to promote relaxation and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression. In traditional Thai medicine, Thai Jasmine is valued for its calming and soothing properties, making it a sought-after ingredient in herbal remedies.
